data.content: No version conflicts — each package appears in only one project. I'll create `Directory.Packages.props` and strip versions from the `.csproj` files.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information. Let me collect all packages:

From Web.csproj:
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting 1.15.0
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 8.0.24

From Core.csproj:
- System.Text.Json 9.0.0

From Tests.csproj:
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.12.0
- xunit 2.9.3
- xunit.runner.visualstudio 2.8.2 (PrivateAssets="all")

No conflicts here - each package appears only once. I need to:


1. Create a `Directory.Packages.props` file at the solution root that centralizes all these package versions and enables centralized version management
2. Then strip out the version attributes from each project file's package references while preserving any other attributes like `PrivateAssets`

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

1. **Created `Directory.Packages.props`** in the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and all 6 packages listed as `<PackageVersion>` entries.

2. **Removed `Version` attributes** from all `<PackageReference>` items in the 3 `.csproj` files, preserving other attributes like `PrivateAssets="all"`.

No version conflicts existed — each package appeared in only one project.
